hawkish_phrases: lack of further progress toward the Committee's 2 percent inflation objective
does not expect it will be appropriate to reduce the target range until it has gained greater confidence that inflation is moving sustainably toward 2 percent
risks to inflation were seen as tilted to the upside
Various participants mentioned a willingness to tighten policy further should risks to inflation materialize
many participants commented on their uncertainty about the degree of restrictiveness
dovish_phrases: risks to achieving the Committee's employment and inflation goals have moved toward better balance
signs that the finances of low- and moderate-income households were increasingly coming under pressure
reducing policy restraint in the event of an unexpected weakening in labor market conditions
slowing the pace of decline of the Federal Reserve's securities holdings
summary: The Committee voted unanimously to hold the target range at 5.25-5.50 percent, explicitly acknowledging a lack of further progress on inflation and stating it does not expect rate cuts until gaining greater confidence inflation is moving sustainably toward 2 percent, with some participants willing to tighten further if needed.
